# The coordination network toolkit: a framework for detecting and analysing coordinated behaviour on social media

**Authors:** Timothy Graham, Sam Hames, Elizabeth Alpert  
**Year:** 2024  
**URL:** https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s42001-024-00260-z  
**Evidence type:** Open-source methodological framework

## Claim supported

Coordination can be modelled as directed weighted multi-behaviour graphs.

## Key result

The toolkit builds networks where accounts are connected when they repeatedly perform same or similar actions in a short time window.

## How it supports manufactured traction

This source strengthens the argument that digital systems can be moved by manipulated proxy signals. Use it as evidence for the mechanism, not as proof that every campaign succeeds.
